description:
Context:
    You are acting as a support engineer who has just been granted terminal access to a customer’s Linux machine.  
    The customer has already collected three diagnostic files in the directory  
        /home/user/support_source  
    and asked you to “sync them to your workspace and provide a short, reproducible log”.

    Your objective is to:
    1. Transfer (via rsync) every file that currently exists in /home/user/support_source into a new destination directory that must be named  
           /home/user/support_collect  
       (create the destination directory if it does not exist).
    2. Produce a single-session text log that follows the exact, line-by-line format described below.  
       The log must be saved at:  
           /home/user/diagnostic_sync/sync.log  

    Required log layout (every line must match literally; no extra spaces or blank lines):
        Line-1:  SYNC SESSION START
        Line-2:  SOURCE: /home/user/support_source
        Line-3:  DEST: /home/user/support_collect
        Line-4:  FILES COPIED:
        Line-5+: one file name per line, in strict ASCII-alphabetical order, exactly as the file appears in the source directory (do NOT include the directory path)
        Last line:  SYNC SESSION END

    Example (you will have different byte counts and should not include them):
        SYNC SESSION START
        SOURCE: /home/user/support_source
        DEST: /home/user/support_collect
        FILES COPIED:
        config.conf
        status.json
        system.log
        SYNC SESSION END

    Additional requirements:
    • Use rsync for the copy step.  
    • The log must be created after the rsync finishes; overwrite any existing log.  
    • Verify that the destination directory now contains an identical copy of every file that was present in /home/user/support_source.  
    • Only the log file should be written inside /home/user/diagnostic_sync; no other files go there.  

    Hints (do NOT output these commands verbatim):
    • You will need about 3–4 commands total: create destination directory, create log directory, run rsync, then build the log with echo or printf plus an `ls -1` for the alphabetical list.  
    • Remember to create /home/user/diagnostic_sync before writing the log.  
    • Append to the log in the order shown above, or compose it in a temporary file and move it into place.  

    Completion criteria checked by the automated grader:
    1. /home/user/support_collect exists and contains exactly the same files (and byte-for-byte identical content) as /home/user/support_source.
    2. /home/user/diagnostic_sync/sync.log exists, is readable by the current user, and matches the line-by-line format specified above.
truth:
Initial environment present when the task starts:

    1. Directory tree:
        /home/user/support_source/
            ├── config.conf
            ├── status.json
            └── system.log

        The directories /home/user/support_collect and /home/user/diagnostic_sync do NOT yet exist.

    2. Exact contents of each source file (byte-for-byte, POSIX newlines):

        /home/user/support_source/config.conf
            max_connections=200
            log_level=debug

        /home/user/support_source/status.json
            {
              "service": "web-api",
              "status": "running",
              "uptime": 43200
            }

        /home/user/support_source/system.log
            2024-01-01 00:00:01 INFO System boot
            2024-01-01 00:00:05 INFO Diagnostics initialized
            2024-01-01 00:10:12 WARN High memory usage detected

    3. Expected final state after correct task execution:

        a) /home/user/support_collect exists and contains three files whose contents are byte-for-byte identical to those listed above.

        b) /home/user/diagnostic_sync/sync.log content (exact, including line breaks):

            SYNC SESSION START
            SOURCE: /home/user/support_source
            DEST: /home/user/support_collect
            FILES COPIED:
            config.conf
            status.json
            system.log
            SYNC SESSION END

        c) File and directory permissions: all created files should have at least user read (u+r) permission; directories should have u+rx.

    The grader will:
        • Compare checksums of files in /home/user/support_source and /home/user/support_collect.  
        • Read /home/user/diagnostic_sync/sync.log and validate it matches the 7-line layout shown above exactly.
